With uncertainty around rate cuts and pressure in private credit, I’m watching this closely.

⸻

📉 Bearish Scenario

data.title: JP Morgan (JPM) - Bullish Reversal & Market Structure Shift
data.description:
Technical Analysis Overview 
On the 1H timeframe, JPM has shown a significant reaction after tapping into the Demand Zone (blue box) near the $284.00 - $288.00 level. After clearing the sell-side liquidity at the Weak Low, we are seeing a strong bullish impulse.
 
Key Technical Points: 
Liquidity Purge & Reaction: The price successfully swept the liquidity below the previous swing lows and found strong buying interest in the primary demand area.

 Market Structure:  We have seen a clear CHoCH (Change of Character) on the internal structure. The current price action suggests that the bearish momentum is exhausted, and a new bullish trend is forming.

 Path of Least Resistance:  I expect the price to follow a zig-zag recovery pattern, potentially retesting the $308.00 minor supply area before continuing its move toward the major liquidity pools above.

 Primary Target:  My main objective is the Strong High and the Supply Zone (red box) at the $324.00 - $326.00 range. Beyond that, the unmitigated gaps near $332.00 remain long-term targets.

 Trading Strategy: 
Bias: Bullish

 Target Levels : $310.00 (Partial TP), $324.00 (Main Target).

 Invalidation:  A break and close below the Weak Low ($278.00) would invalidate this bullish thesis.
 
Disclaimer:  This is an analysis of market structure and not financial advice. Always manage your risk properly, especially with banking sector volatility.

data.author: SIMMONSCAPITAL

data.title: Head & Shoulders triggered
data.description:
Bearish looks to move lower 

Key Risks That Could Push JPMorgan (JPM) Lower

1️⃣ Rising expenses and investment spending

JPM expects ~$105B in expenses for 2026, above previous estimates.

Higher spending on AI, branches, marketing, and compensation could pressure margins.

2️⃣ Economic slowdown / credit cycle risk

If the economy weakens, banks face:

higher loan defaults

lower lending demand

reduced investment banking activity.

3️⃣ Interest-rate cycle turning

JPM benefited from high interest rates boosting net interest income.

If rates fall or yield curves compress, bank profitability can decline.

4️⃣ Profit-taking after strong multi-year rally

JPM has rallied strongly over recent years and is still near historical highs.

Large institutional holders (over 70% institutional ownership) may rebalance or lock in gains.

5️⃣ Market volatility hitting trading & dealmaking

Investment banking and trading revenues depend heavily on market activity.

Slower IPO or M&A markets could reduce revenue growth.

6️⃣ Global macro risks

Geopolitical shocks, inflation, or financial market stress could hurt financial stocks broadly.

Even JPM leadership has warned of possible economic turbulence.

✅ In simple terms:
The biggest downside risks for JPM are rising costs, a potential economic slowdown affecting loans and deal activity, and sensitivity to interest-rate changes.
data.author: William_Playfair

data.title: JPMorgan Chase — Long-Term Structural Behaviour | S&P 500 Struct
data.description:
This chart documents the long-term structural behaviour of JPMorgan Chase & Co. as part of the ongoing **S&P 500 Structural Census study.

The project examines structural regimes across companies in the S&P 500 using higher-timeframe price charts.

Rather than forecasting market movements, the objective is to observe how structural patterns evolve across major equities over extended market cycles.

The chart highlights a structural regime transition in the early 1990s, after which the stock entered a sustained expansion phase with periodic cyclical corrections typical of financial-sector stocks.

Structure → Level → Trigger → Probability
data.author: kacraj

data.title: JPMorgan Pullback Reaches Key Support Zone
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase & Co. is one of the largest and most influential financial institutions in the world. Headquartered in New York, the bank operates across investment banking, asset management, commercial banking, and consumer financial services. As a major component of the global financial system, JPMorgan’s stock often reflects broader sentiment in the financial sector and the overall economy.

From a technical perspective, JPMorgan is currently experiencing a healthy pullback after its strong prior advance.

The stock is pulling back nicely after extending lower following a retest of the channel resistance from the outside around 325, a level we discussed previously. That rejection confirmed the resistance area and triggered the current corrective move.

At the moment, price action appears to be unfolding within wave C of the correction, which is part of a broader A-B-C retracement structure. Importantly, the stock is now testing the key support zone between 280 and 290. This level is technically significant because it also aligns with the February 2025 high, making it a strong former resistance that could now act as support.

If the current three-wave decline represents a higher-degree wave four correction, the market may be approaching the later stages of the retracement. Should support hold, this zone could become a foundation for a potential continuation of the broader uptrend.

For traders and investors watching the chart closely, 280–290 stands out as the first major area where bullish reactions could appear.

Highlights:
Three-wave retracement appears to be nearing completion
Price is testing a key support zone between 280–290
Area aligns with the February 2025 high, strengthening the level
Current levels may offer an attractive buying opportunity if the trend resumes

data.author: SIMMONSCAPITAL

data.title: JPMorgan
data.description:
In a significant move that underscores the growing importance of private markets within the wealth management landscape, J.P. Morgan Asset Management has announced the appointment of two seasoned executives to lead and expand its private wealth alternatives business. Stephanie Davis has been named Head of Private Wealth Alternatives, while Sean Flynn has been appointed as Private Wealth Alternatives National Manager. These strategic hires signal the firm's commitment to deepening its support for financial advisors and meeting the surging demand from high-net-worth clients for access to private market investments.

Stephanie Davis: A Proven Leader Joins from Hamilton Lane
Stephanie Davis joins J.P. Morgan Asset Management from Hamilton Lane, a leading global investment firm focused on private markets, where she served as Managing Director and Co-Head of US Private Wealth Solutions. In that capacity, she was responsible for the strategic direction and management of a wide array of private market offerings, including secondaries, co-investments, private credit, infrastructure, and venture capital. Her leadership was instrumental in shaping Hamilton Lane's approach to serving the wealth management channel, a sector that has become increasingly vital for private markets firms seeking to expand their investor base.

Prior to her tenure at Hamilton Lane, Davis headed capital formation efforts at LL Funds (Ultra Capital) , where she honed her expertise in sourcing and structuring investments. Her career trajectory reflects a deep and specialized focus on connecting institutional-quality private market strategies with a broader range of investors.

In her new role at J.P. Morgan, Davis will operate out of New York and report directly to Steve Lundquist, Head of US Advisor at J.P. Morgan Asset Management. She will oversee the firm's entire private wealth alternatives business, which encompasses a range of private markets strategies specifically tailored for financial advisors and their clients. Her mandate extends beyond mere oversight; Davis is expected to play a pivotal role in both developing new private market offerings and expanding the reach of existing ones. A key component of her responsibilities will also involve leading client education initiatives, helping financial advisors and their clients understand the nuances, risks, and potential rewards of allocating capital to illiquid asset classes such as private equity, private credit, and infrastructure.

Sean Flynn: An Internal Promotion to Drive National Engagement
Complementing Davis's external hire is the internal appointment of Sean Flynn as Private Wealth Alternatives National Manager. Flynn is a veteran of J.P. Morgan, having most recently served as Head of Alternative Investments for the firm's US Wealth Management division. In that role, he was responsible for managing alternative investment sales and distribution to the firm's extensive wealth management client base. His experience also includes leading alternative investment distribution and managing partner relations at First Republic Investment Management prior to its acquisition by JPMorgan Chase.

In his new position, Flynn will work in close partnership with Davis. His primary responsibility is to lead and manage a dedicated team of field-based client advisors strategically located across the United States. This team's core mission is to focus on alternative investments education and engagement, acting as the frontline resource for financial advisors seeking to incorporate private markets into client portfolios. The group has ambitious growth plans, aiming to expand to 20 dedicated professionals by the end of the year. This specialized team will be part of the broader US Advisor organization, which is on track to reach a total of 350 staff members , reflecting the significant resources J.P. Morgan is dedicating to this area.

Leadership Commentary: A Commitment to Education and Expansion
Steve Lundquist, Head of US Advisor at J.P. Morgan Asset Management, expressed strong enthusiasm for the new appointments, highlighting the strategic importance of the private wealth alternatives business.

"We are thrilled to welcome Stephanie and Sean on board," Lundquist said in a statement. "They are both proven leaders and will play a critical role in our continued commitment to help educate financial advisors on the rapidly evolving private markets space. Stephanie's and Sean's track records and deep experience specializing in serving this important client segment will accelerate our momentum as investor demand for alternative investments continues to climb."

Lundquist's comments underscore a key trend in the asset management industry: the democratization of private markets. Once the exclusive domain of large institutional investors like pension funds and endowments, private market strategies are increasingly being made available to high-net-worth individuals. This shift requires a significant educational effort, as these investments differ markedly from traditional public stocks and bonds in terms of liquidity, valuation, and risk profile. The appointments of Davis and Flynn, along with the expansion of the field-based team, are direct responses to this need.

J.P. Morgan's Substantial Footprint in Alternatives
The firm's push into private wealth alternatives is backed by a substantial and established presence in the asset class. J.P. Morgan Asset Management reports over $600 billion in alternative assets under management across a diverse range of sectors. This extensive portfolio includes:

Real Estate: A global platform spanning private and public real estate equity and debt.

Private Equity: Direct investments and fund investments across buyout, growth equity, and venture capital.

Private Credit: A leading direct lending franchise providing financing to middle-market companies.

Infrastructure: Investments in essential assets such as transportation, energy, and digital infrastructure.

Hedge Funds: A comprehensive fund of funds and co-investment platform.

This deep and broad alternative investment capability provides the foundation upon which Davis and Flynn will build their private wealth offerings.

A Note on Broader Corporate Developments
This leadership expansion comes at a time of broader evolution within J.P. Morgan Asset Management. Earlier this year, as reported by the Wall Street Journal, the firm announced it would be discontinuing its use of traditional proxy advisory firms. Instead, it is transitioning to an in-house system that leverages artificial intelligence to handle shareholder voting decisions. This move reflects a broader trend among large asset managers to take more direct and data-driven control over their stewardship responsibilities.

Conclusion: Positioning for the Future of Wealth Management
The appointments of Stephanie Davis and Sean Flynn represent a strategic and well-resourced effort by J.P. Morgan Asset Management to solidify its leadership in the rapidly growing private wealth alternatives space. By combining external expertise with internal talent, and by building a dedicated national education and engagement team, the firm is positioning itself to meet the evolving needs of financial advisors and their clients. As investor demand for private markets continues its upward trajectory, J.P. Morgan's commitment to this area signals a clear belief that alternatives will play an increasingly central role in the future of wealth management.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) – Daily Timeframe Technical Analysis
data.description:
Analysis Date: 24 February 2026
Current Price at Time of Analysis: 297.67 

Technical Structure

JPM has formed a clear Head & Shoulders topping pattern on the daily timeframe:

Left Shoulder: ~318–320

Head: ~335–338

Right Shoulder: ~323–326

Neckline support: rising trendline now breaking down

Price is currently trading below the right shoulder resistance, indicating weakening bullish momentum and increasing downside pressure.

Primary Bearish Move
🎯 First Downside Target: 264 – 259

This zone represents:

Major horizontal support

Previous consolidation base

Strong demand area

A continuation move lower toward 264–259 is technically justified following the topping structure breakdown.

Corrective Rebound Scenario

After reaching the 264–259 demand zone, a technical rebound is likely.

🔄 Potential Pullback Zone: 292 – 302

This area serves as:

Previous support turned resistance

Supply zone

Lower high structure area

A corrective rally into 292–302 would be considered a retest of broken structure rather than a bullish reversal.

Continuation Bearish Scenario

If price rejects from 292–302, the broader pattern suggests:

Resumption of downside pressure

Continuation toward deeper corrective levels

Potential measured move completion of the Head & Shoulders

The 292–302 zone is therefore a key decision area.

Invalidation Level

Stop Loss: Above 326

A sustained move above 326 would invalidate the bearish structure and cancel the Head & Shoulders scenario.

Summary Outlook

Short-term bias: Bearish toward 264–259

Expected reaction: Technical rebound toward 292–302

Medium-term bias: Bearish continuation if rejected from 292–302

Invalidation: Above 326
data.author: IbrahimTarek

data.title: Strong OBV Inflow Leading The Move
data.description:
JPMorgan is pushing higher with a confirmed breakout backed by the strongest signal in this setup — heavy OBV inflow. The bias is solidly bullish and the bounce has already recovered nearly 8 times the retrace. The question is whether volume follows the accumulation signal.

Price is at 310.35 after a minimal -0.6% retrace with a 4.6% bounce at 7.6x expansion, reading as breakout-plus. Price percentile sits at 34.7% in the lower portion of the 335.73 to 296.87 range. Despite the strong breakout reading, price still has substantial room to the upside within its historical range.

Bias is strong bull at 66.1% with a 37:19 signal count out of 112. EMAs are clean at 7:1, candles favor bulls at 10:4, and counter-trend signals read 11:3. Ichimoku is deadlocked at 7:7 which is the only area of structural hesitation. Spread strength is 32.1% in the strong zone. Star formations read 1:0 and DD/SS prints 7:3. Harami and pattern totals lean slightly bearish at 1:3 and 2:3 respectively, showing some indecision candles are forming within the broader bullish structure.

No squeeze is active with bandwidth at 3.56%. Momentum is bullish and pointing upward. Squeeze momentum is contracting downward at 407.7% which creates a tension against the bullish momentum direction. This contraction could mean the advance is consolidating before the next leg rather than accelerating.

Volume is quiet at -0.82 Z on just 717 contracts and 222.52K in dollar terms. Direction reads neutral with decelerating momentum at -1.42. Bull and bear Z scores sit at -0.59 and -0.42, both subdued. No whale activity present.

The defining signal is OBV at 2.1 reading strong inflow with normal divergence. This is significant accumulation happening beneath quiet surface volume. Institutions are building positions without pushing price aggressively, which is a hallmark of smart money accumulation. The disconnect between quiet volume Z and strong OBV inflow suggests controlled buying rather than speculative chasing.

The 34.7% price percentile with a breakout regime is an interesting combination. It means the breakout is happening from a relatively low position within the range, giving the move room to run before hitting the 335.73 high. This is healthier than a breakout at the 90th percentile where exhaustion risk is high.

Scenario 1 (60%) — Continuation toward 335 and the range high. The 37:19 signal count, 7.6x breakout expansion, and OBV inflow at 2.1 all support higher prices. The 34.7% price percentile provides substantial upside runway. A volume pickup confirming the OBV accumulation would accelerate the move. EMAs at 7:1 and counter-trend at 11:3 give bulls strong structural control.

Scenario 2 (40%) — Consolidation and pullback to the 300-305 area. The 7:7 Ichimoku deadlock, 2:3 pattern total lean, and contracting squeeze momentum suggest the advance could stall. If OBV inflow fades and the quiet volume profile persists, the breakout could fail to sustain. Watch for harami formations to increase as early warning of momentum exhaustion.

Watch for volume Z to confirm the OBV story by breaking above zero. The Ichimoku deadlock at 7:7 resolving in either direction will signal whether the trend has structural follow-through. A squeeze forming and firing upward from here would add significant fuel.

Risk is moderate with favorable asymmetry. The breakout is confirmed with strong accumulation and room to run in the range. Downside is defined near 296.87. The main concern is the quiet volume profile not matching the OBV conviction. Let price hold above 305 as the trend continuation trigger.

More analysis on my profile.

JPM, JPMorgan, banking, finance, breakout, volume, OBV, equities

data.author: SIMMONSCAPITAL

data.title: $JPM – Liquidity Cleaned, Expansion Next?
data.description:
One of the names I’ve been tracking this week is JPM.

After the recent pullback into ~$296, price swept short-term sell-side liquidity without reaching the deeper $290 pocket. Since then, we’ve seen stabilization above $300 and volume starting to rotate back in.

This doesn’t look like panic selling.
It looks more like positioning.

At the moment, price is working inside a decision area between roughly $300–314. There’s still an imbalance overhead near $314 that hasn’t been fully repaired. If we continue seeing steady bid flow and acceptance above $300, the next logical magnet becomes the upper liquidity pool around $323–324.

On the downside, I’d only get cautious if we lose $297 with acceptance. Until then, structure is still intact.

For those who trade options, one conservative idea could be a bull put spread around the 285 / 280 strikes (short-term expiry). If JPM holds structure and doesn’t revisit lower liquidity, this type of spread can offer roughly ~10-15% return on risk over a 2–3 week window depending on fills and IV.

The simpler approach is buying shares around current levels and targeting a controlled 5–6% move toward the $320s if liquidity above gets taken.

This isn’t about indicators for me.
It’s about:

• Liquidity sweep completed
• Structure holding above $300
• Imbalance overhead
• Quiet institutional rotation

For now, I’m tracking and waiting for continued confirmation through volume and acceptance.

As always, these are personal trading notes, not a recommendation.
Do your own analysis and manage risk properly.

If you have any questions, feedback, or want to understand my approach in more depth, feel free to reach out or drop a comment. I answer every question for free, your feedback helps me improve, and we both learn along the way. Likes, and comments are super appreciated too! :) 

#777BlessTrading

data.author: Stanislav_Bernukhov_Exness

data.title: JP MORGAN topped on its 17-year Channel Up. Bear Cycle started.
data.description:
JP Morgan Chase (JPM) has been trading within a 17-year Channel Up since the March 2009 market bottom of the U.S. Housing Crisis. Last month the price hit the exact top of that multi-year pattern and got rejected. Despite the recent rebound, we expect that to be short-lived, as it's not just the Head and Shoulders (H&S) pattern that is being formed but that Channel Up Top alone calls for a strong, potentially 1-year long Bear Cycle.

Such have historically been most major corrections within this pattern, with the last two (2022 Inflation Crisis and 2020 COVID crash) bottomed near the 1M MA100 (red trend-line), which is the utmost long-term Support and the before (2015) bottoming on the 1W MA200 (orange trend-line).

With the 1W RSI currently on a huge Bearish Divergence (Lower Highs against price's Higher Highs) since March 2024, we expect the stock to drop to at least $215.00, which like 2015 will make contact with both the 1W MA200 and the 0.382 Fibonacci retracement level. At most, if the market repeats the -41.90% decline of 2022, we expect it to hit $195.00 just above the 1M MA100.

A deeper correction (which is quite unlikely based on the current fundamentals) would see JPM target the 0.618 Fib, which is where the 2022 and 2020 corrections bottomed, at $160.00. 

---

data.description:
JPM is trading inside a well-defined ascending channel after a strong bullish rally.
Price has now pulled back sharply and is currently testing the lower boundary of the channel, making this a critical decision zone.

 Technical Structure
 	•	Primary trend: Bullish (as long as channel support holds)
	•	Pattern: Ascending Channel
	•	Moving Average: Price slightly below MA → short-term bearish pressure

 Key Levels
 	•	Major Support:
295 – 298 (Channel bottom)
	•	Next Supports if broken:
285 → 275
	•	Resistances:
315 → 325 → 335

 Bullish Scenario (Primary)
 
If:
	•	Price holds above 295–298
	•	Reclaims the channel structure

➡️ This move would be considered a healthy pullback within an uptrend.

Upside Targets:
315 → 325 → Upper channel (~335)

 Bearish Scenario (Alternative)
 
If:
	•	Daily close below the channel support

➡️ Bullish structure weakens and deeper correction becomes likely.

Downside Targets:
285 → 275

 Final Takeaway
 
JPM is at make-or-break support.
Holding the channel keeps the bullish trend alive; losing it opens the door to a deeper correction.
